refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation, task scheduling, and resource allocation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers (equipment operation efficiency, task scheduling, resource allocation) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and verify that each sub-FR contributes to meeting or exceeding the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation efficiency to maximize output per hour.
FR2: Schedule tasks effectively to minimize idle time and maximize utilization.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to support continuous productivity.

candidate_dps: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms to improve operation efficiency.
DP2: Intelligent task scheduling system to optimize work sequences.
DP3: Dynamic resource allocation framework integrating telemetry and tasking data.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms to improve operation efficiency.

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and task scheduling modules.
Dependency on telemetry data for real-time adjustments.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically decoupled from task scheduling and resource allocation but requires telemetry input; interfaces are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry data is accurate and timely.
Risk that vehicle control improvements alone may not meet productivity target without scheduling and resource allocation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to address full productivity scope.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Realization of advanced vehicle control algorithms including embedded control software and hardware interfaces.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time vehicle and environment data for control adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands to align vehicle operation with scheduled tasks.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication with other modules and remote monitoring.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as vehicle control logic is centralized here; low diffusion risk.
Broader scope; less focused containment for vehicle control.
Poor containment; vehicle control logic does not belong here.
No containment; unrelated to vehicle control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing vehicle control algorithms within the Vehicle module minimizes diffusion and supports clear ownership and interface management.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Tasking and Telemetry modules if requirements evolve.
Risk of overloading Vehicle module if additional functionalities are added without clear boundaries.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface complexity and consider modularizing control algorithms if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (advanced vehicle control algorithms) logically satisfies the refined FR (optimize equipment operation efficiency) and is verifiable.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as vehicle control logic is well-contained within Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Vehicle as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and productivity measurements during operations.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of control algorithms to Vehicle module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Plan for integration of task scheduling and resource allocation DPs in next decomposition level.
Review interface definitions periodically to manage evolving requirements.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound for the selected DP and IM, the overall productivity FR requires further decomposition to fully address all productivity drivers (task scheduling and resource allocation).
next_level_sub_frs: FR2: Schedule tasks effectively to minimize idle time and maximize utilization.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to support continuous productivity.

revise.why: To include additional sub-FRs covering task scheduling and resource allocation for comprehensive productivity support.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Vehicle control improvements significantly impact productivity.
open_risks: Integration complexity with task scheduling and resource allocation modules.
Potential interface evolution increasing diffusion risk.
evidence_needed: Operational productivity data validating vehicle control impact.
Interface performance metrics between Vehicle and Tasking modules.
